data.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Doubles Down on 'Santa Claus Rally,' Sees Stocks Rising Into Year's End
data.description:
Analysts at Goldman Sachs (GS) still believe in Santa Claus.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Wall Street investment bank says that, despite a lackluster start to December, they still expect there to be a "Santa Claus rally" this year and for stocks to move higher into year's end. The S&P 500 index rose 0.8% on Dec. 18, snapping a four-day losing streak that has left the benchmark down for the month.

However, history has shown that December is a resilient month for stocks and that equities usually end the holiday period higher. Since 1928, the S&P 500 has risen 75% of the time in the last two weeks of December, posting an average gain of 1.3% during that timeframe, according to market data.

Santa Claus Is Coming to Wall Street

In a note to clients, Goldman Sachs says that, while longer-term worries about the artificial-intelligence (AI) trade linger and valuations remain stretched, optimism over a strong economy and corporate profits is keeping investor sentiment upbeat heading into 2026.

"Barring any major shocks, it will be hard to fight the overwhelmingly positive seasonal period we are entering and the cleaner positioning set-up," wrote Goldman Sachs. "While we don't necessarily see a dramatic rally, we do think there is room to go up from here into year end."

U.S. stocks continue to trend higher on Dec. 19, buoyed by a cooler-than-expected inflation report for November, which has bolstered prospects for more interest-rate cuts in the new year.

Is GS Stock a Buy?

Goldman Sachs' stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 14 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on six Buy and eight Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GS price target of $841 implies 6.07% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Musk and Trump Make Up for Xmas in Potential SpaceX Land Swap Deal
data.description:
Elon Musk's bromance with President Trump might be back on for Christmas in the form of a land swap deal for the tech tycoon's space exploration business SpaceX (PC.SPXEX)

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SpaceX Eyes Expansion

According to a report in the New York Times, the Trump administration is reportedly considering giving nearly 800 acres of land in a federal wildlife refuge in Texas to SpaceX, the rocket and satellite maker run by Musk.

The Times, citing documents it had reviewed said that, SpaceX, in return, would give the government around 692 acres of property it has purchased elsewhere in Cameron County, Texas some of which is about 20 miles from the refuge.

SpaceX will use the land to expand its rocket launch and production site in Cameron County.

Under the proposed deal, the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service would give SpaceX the land, which is currently part of the Lower Rio Grande Valley National Wildlife Refuge.

The report said it is unclear whether the deal would require SpaceX to take steps to protect wildlife, habitats, or any cultural resources linked to the land it receives from the government.

Good Timing for IPO

This deal is not unique. The report said that Republicans and Democrats have previously exchanged land in wildlife refuges with private companies. Last year, the Biden administration gave utility companies nearly 20 acres of public land in the Upper Mississippi River National Wildlife Refuge.

However, it is interesting given SpaceX's plans to IPO, potentially as early as next year, as it continues its mission to land on Mars, launch more satellites and even build AI data centers in space.

It is also notable given the relationship between Elon Musk and President Trump. It looked like a beneficial and friendly one at the start of the year when Musk's support for Trump in his re-election bid ended in him getting a job in his administration.

However, the pair later fell out in acrimonious circumstances over Trump's spending plans and lack of support for EVs.

Is TSLA a Good Stock to Buy Now?

As SpaceX is still a private company let's look at EV maker Tesla (TSLA) where Elon Musk is chief executive. On TipRanks, TSLA has a Hold consensus based on 11 Buy,12 Hold and 9 Sell ratings. Its highest price target is $600. TSLA stock's consensus price target is $385.34, implying a 21.15% downside.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: SoundHound AI (SOUN) Stock Forecast: Top Analysts Remain Bullish for 2026
data.description:
SoundHound AI (SOUN) has been attracting attention from Wall Street analysts heading into 2026. The company continues to expand its technology offerings, partnerships, and market presence, fueling optimism about its long-term growth potential. While investors weigh risks such as competition and execution challenges, top analysts believe in SoundHound's long-term potential, making it a stock to watch closely.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, SoundHound operates as a voice AI platform that enables brands to engage with users through customized, voice-driven products, services, and applications. The company currently has a market capitalization of approximately $4.63 billion.

Cantor Upgrades SOUN Stock to Buy

Cantor Fitzgerald's four-star analyst Thomas Blakey recently upgraded SOUN stock from Hold to Buy and raised his price target to $15 from $13. Blakey cited SoundHound AI's strong execution, especially its success in cross-selling and upselling voice and conversational AI solutions to existing customers.

Blakey further stated that expectations for SoundHound AI remain relatively low heading into 2026, which could leave room for upside if the company keeps executing on its growth plans. He highlighted that the company's recent Interactions acquisition, completed in September 2025, is a key driver of organic growth.

He also pointed to the Consumer Electronics Show in early January 2026 as a potential catalyst for SOUN, noting that any new Voice Commerce announcements at the event could help drive the stock higher.

Other Analysts Weigh In On SOUN Stock

Likewise, Mizuho Securities also upgraded SOUN stock to Buy and lifted its price target to $15.

Similarly, four-star-rated analyst Michael Legg at Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. reiterated his Buy rating on SOUN, predicting an upside of 45% from current levels.

What Is the Price Target for SOUN?

According to TipRanks, SOUN stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with five Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. The average SoundHound stock price target is $17.33, suggesting a potential upside of 53.7% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: TSLA, RKLB, OKLO, ACHR: Cathie Wood Trims Tesla and Rocket Lab, Loads Up on Oklo and Archer Aviation Stocks
data.description:
Cathie Wood made several trades across her ARK ETFs on Monday, December 22, according to daily trade filings. The moves showed continued selling in Tesla (TSLA), the electric-vehicle maker, and fresh cuts in Rocket Lab (RKLB), the space launch company. At the same time, ARK added to positions in Oklo (OKLO), a nuclear energy firm, Archer Aviation (ACHR), an electric aircraft developer, and several biotech stocks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Is Cathie Wood Selling Tesla and Rocket Lab Stocks?

On the sell side, ARK continued to scale back its position in Tesla. The firm sold 60,715 shares of TSLA, valued at about $29.2 million. The move follows last week's sale of more than 124,000 shares, signaling a gradual reduction in exposure rather than a full exit.

Tesla stock has been volatile, as investors weigh progress in autonomous driving against valuation concerns after the recent rally. Even so, shares finished Monday up 1.56% at $488.73, showing that investor interest remains strong even as ARK locked in some gains.

ARK also cut back its stake in Rocket Lab, selling 232,425 shares worth roughly $16.4 million. The move marks a notable reduction in the space stock as the fund appears to be capitalizing on the recent surge in RKLB shares. Notably, Rocket Lab stock hit a new 52-week high on Monday as investor confidence rose around its faster launch pace, new government contracts, and a larger role in the commercial space market. With shares up more than 205% year to date, ARK's sale suggests profit-taking after a strong run.

Beyond Tesla and Rocket Lab, ARK also reduced exposure to consumer and streaming technology names. The fund sold 33,164 shares of Shopify (SHOP) for about $5.6 million and 40,217 shares of Roku (ROKU) worth roughly $4.4 million, pointing to ongoing portfolio rebalancing within its technology holdings.

Let's see how the stocks ARK sold stack up on TipRanks.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: RKLB Stock Forecast: Needham Hikes Rocket Lab Price Target to Street-High $90
data.description:
Shares of Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) earned a Street-high price target of $90 from Needham's five-star-rated analyst Ryan Koontz. Koontz raised his target from $63 and reiterated his Buy rating after the company secured a major contract with the U.S. Space Development Agency (SDA), highlighting Rocket Lab's growing position in the commercial and government launch markets. Following the contract, RKLB stock surged 10% on Monday, but is down 6% in pre-market hours today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Rocket Lab is a space launch company that focuses on small rockets and satellite solutions, including its Electron rocket and the upcoming Neutron vehicle. The company's Neutron is a next-generation, partially reusable vehicle built to carry medium-to-large satellites into orbit. It will compete in the commercial space market by providing cost-effective launch solutions.

Why Needham Lifted Price Target on RKLB

Needham's Koontz highlighted Rocket Lab's largest-ever contract from the SDA, which doubles the company's Space Systems backlog. He believes this contract is a strong vote of confidence in Rocket Lab's capabilities as a defense contractor. Koontz also pointed out the potential upside from supplying satellites and components to other contractors, which could raise the total opportunity to about $1 billion.

Additionally, Koontz notes that this award came after a competitive and delayed process, highlighting Rocket Lab's strong position among established defense peers.

Meanwhile, with Tranche 2 underway, Tranche 3 ramping up, and the first Neutron rocket launch approaching, Koontz sees several near- and mid-term catalysts for revenue growth. He stated that these developments also give better visibility into future defense-related cash flows.

Is RKLB a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, RKLB stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with nine Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Rocket Lab is $68.25, suggesting a potential downside of 12% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Wall Street Looks East: China's AI IPO Surge Offers a New Play for Investors
data.description:
Global investors are moving money out of high-priced U.S. tech stocks and into China's AI sector, with a focus on the fast-growing Chinese chipmakers and tech firms, especially after two blockbuster IPOs this month. Moore Threads jumped 425% in its Shanghai debut, while MetaX Integrated Circuits surged as much as 755%. Both firms were founded by former Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) executives and are now regarded as key components of China's effort to build its own chip supply.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The shift comes as Beijing increases its support for homegrown AI and chip companies. It is also speeding up IPOs for firms working in these fields. Meanwhile, many investors are growing concerned about the rich valuations of AI stocks in the U.S. For comparison, the Nasdaq index trades at 31 times earnings, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng Tech Index sits at 24 times earnings. The lower valuation in China is drawing more attention, especially as its companies try to catch up on both software and chipmaking.

Institutional Funds Rebalance Exposure

Large asset managers are rethinking their portfolios. U.K.-based investment firm Ruffer has cut back exposure to the top U.S. tech firms and is increasing its stake in Alibaba Group (BABA). The company owns its own AI chip unit and a large language model called Qwen. It is also spending more to grow its cloud systems.

At the same time, the KraneShares China Innovation ETF has risen nearly 66% this year to almost $9 billion in assets. The fund includes companies like Baidu (BIDU), Tencent Holdings (TCEHY), and Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC). Another fund from Rayliant, a U.S. investment firm, gives investors access to newer players like Cambricon Technologies, which designs chips used for AI tasks.

UBS Global Wealth Management recently rated Chinese tech stocks as "most attractive." The bank cited strong government policy, faster AI growth, and a need for geographic balance in investor portfolios.

Caution Around Valuations and Profitability

Still, some managers warn that risks remain. Many Chinese chip companies are not yet profitable. Moore Threads, for example, was priced at 123 times next year's sales.

These concerns have raised questions about whether recent gains are driven by fundamentals or by short-term momentum. At the same time, others note that staying selective and maintaining exposure to both global tech leaders and China's emerging AI names may offer a more balanced approach in today's fast-moving and fragmented chip landscape.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to assemble notable Chinese companies publicly traded on Wall Street. This tool helps investors gain an in-depth look at each stock and the broader Chinese tech industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: BitMine Stock Jumps as Tom Lee Says 'We Are Already Seeing the Synergies Borne from our Substantial ETH Holdings'
data.description:
BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) shares climbed to $31.21 in Tuesday's session, holding steady as the company officially crossed the historic 4 million ETH milestone. Helmed by Fundstrat's Tom Lee, the firm has solidified its position as the world's largest corporate Ethereum treasury, now controlling 3.37% of the total supply. Tom Lee said: "We are making rapid progress towards the 'alchemy of 5%' and we are already seeing the synergies borne from our substantial ETH holdings."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

With over $12.2 billion in Ether now sitting in its vault, BitMine is aggressively executing its "Alchemy of 5%" target. This is a strategic play designed to create structural scarcity and position the company as the primary institutional bridge to the blockchain.

Tom Lee Sprints Past the Four Million Token Milestone

While broader markets remain cautious, BitMine's chairman has maintained a high "velocity of accumulation," deploying $40 million in the latest round of purchases. This newest acquisition of 13,412 ETH takes the company's total haul over the past week to nearly 100,000 tokens, achieving a goal Lee described as a "tremendous milestone" reached in just under six months.

By maintaining an average purchase price of $2,991 per ETH, the firm has successfully moved its massive treasury back into the green, leveraging the recent recovery in crypto prices to bolster its net asset value.

MAVAN Staking Network Prepares to Unlock Yield in 2026

The next phase of the BitMine evolution centers on the "Made in America Validator Network" (MAVAN). Set for deployment in early 2026, this staking infrastructure is designed to transform the firm's passive treasury into a high-margin revenue stream.

Analysts suggest that by converting idle tokens into active validators, BitMine could catapult into the ranks of the most profitable publicly traded firms in the U.S. This shift from simple holding to active network participation is intended to provide the fundamental earnings needed to support a triple-digit stock price in the long term.

Key Takeaway

The bottom line is that BitMine is transforming into a foundational piece of the global financial OS. By hoarding over 3% of the supply and preparing a secure, American-made staking solution, Tom Lee is betting that institutional demand for yield will eventually drive a supply squeeze. If the MAVAN launch stays on schedule for Q1 2026, the current $31 share price may look like a significant discount compared to the $47 analyst target.

Is BitMine a Good Stock to Buy Now?

BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) is currently maintaining a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on a limited analyst pool. According to the latest data, there is currently only 1 Wall Street analyst offering a 12-month price target for the company within the last three months. This lone rating is a Buy, with no analysts currently suggesting a Hold or Sell position.

The average 12-month price target for BitMine Immersion Technologies is currently set at a flat $47.00. This represents a potential 51.2% upside from the stock's last recorded price.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Trend Lower After Monday's Strong Start
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were in the red on Tuesday morning after a strong start to the holiday-shortened trading week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.07%, 0.05%, and 0.06%, respectively, at 8:00 a.m. EST on December 23.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) will close early at 1 p.m. EST on Wednesday for Christmas Eve and will remain closed on Thursday for Christmas Day.

During Monday's regular trading, the S&P 500 advanced for the third straight session, up 0.64%, helped by a 1.5% rise in Nvidia (NVDA) and gains in Micron (MU) and Oracle (ORCL) stocks. The Dow Jones and Nasdaq Composite were up 0.47% and 0.52%, respectively.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of Danish pharma giant Novo Nordisk (NVO) jumped 8% in Tuesday's pre-market trading as the U.S. FDA approved the company's Wegovy weight loss pill. Moreover, Zim Integrated Shipping Services (ZIM) stock rallied more than 9% as the company confirmed receiving multiple competitive acquisition proposals.

data.title: 'Another Software Fix': Amazon's Zoox Recalls 332 U.S. Vehicles, Regulator Says
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) self-driving unit Zoox is recalling 332 vehicles in the U.S. after a software issue surfaced during testing, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA). The regulator said the issue has occurred more than once and could raise the risk of a crash in certain situations.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Zoox Recalls Vehicles with Older Software

NHTSA said the recall covers vehicles running automated driving system software released before December 19. At or near intersections, the vehicles may cross the yellow center line or drive into or stop in front of oncoming traffic, which increases crash risk.

Zoox said the issue has already been fixed with a software update, provided free of charge. The company added that the update improves how vehicles stay within their lane and respond to traffic near intersections.

Recall Follows Earlier Software Update

This is not the first time Zoox has issued a software-related recall. In May, the company recalled 270 vehicles following a crash in San Francisco. That update focused on improving how vehicles track nearby pedestrians and prevent movement when someone is close.

Such actions are common in self-driving programs that are still in testing.

For Amazon, Zoox remains a long-term project and a small part of the broader business. The recall does not affect Amazon's retail operations or its cloud unit, Amazon Web Services. Still, safety-related headlines around autonomous driving tend to draw attention, even when the direct financial impact is limited.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy? 

AMZN has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 45 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 44 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average price target on AMZN stock of $296.12 implies 29.6% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
